title: glyceria × burdonii
intro: Glyceria × burdonii, also known as Burdon's mannagrass, is a hybrid species of grass within the family Poaceae, resulting from the cross between Glyceria fluitans and Glyceria borealis. The medicinal properties of Glyceria × burdonii lie in its ability to act as a diuretic, helping to reduce water retention and alleviate symptoms of hypertension. The health benefits of consuming Glyceria × burdonii include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, regulate blood sugar, and support digestive health. Herbal preparations of Glyceria × burdonii can be made into teas, infusions, or dried extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions when used under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
